SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.util.heattransfer

Class VesselHeatTransferCorrelations

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.util.heattransfer.VesselHeatTransferCorrelations
public final class VesselHeatTransferCorrelations extends Object
Internal heat-transfer correlations for the gas-to-wall film coefficient inside pressure vessels.

The class provides dimensionless correlations used by transient vessel models:

  • Natural convection (Churchill-Chu) for the quiescent gas inside a vessel during blowdown / depressurization, where the internal flow is buoyancy driven.
  • Mixed convection during filling (Woodfield et al.) for a vessel being charged through an inlet jet, where the jet-induced forced convection and the buoyancy both contribute.

All methods are static and operate purely on dimensionless groups and SI fluid properties, so they are decoupled from the NeqSim thermodynamic objects and can be reused by any transient model.

References: Churchill and Chu (1975), Int. J. Heat Mass Transfer 18, 1323; Woodfield, Monde and Mitsutake (2007), "Measurement of averaged heat transfer coefficients in high-pressure vessel during charging with hydrogen, nitrogen or argon gas", J. Therm. Sci. Tech. 2(2), 180.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Method Details

    • rayleigh

      public static double rayleigh(double thermalExpansionCoeff, double temperatureDifferenceK, double characteristicLengthM, double kinematicViscosity, double thermalDiffusivity)
      Rayleigh number for natural convection.

      Ra = g * beta * |dT| * L^3 / (nu * alpha)

      Parameters:
      thermalExpansionCoeff - isobaric thermal expansion coefficient beta in 1/K; for an ideal gas this is 1/T
      temperatureDifferenceK - absolute temperature difference between surface and bulk in K
      characteristicLengthM - characteristic length L in m
      kinematicViscosity - kinematic viscosity nu in m^2/s; must be positive
      thermalDiffusivity - thermal diffusivity alpha in m^2/s; must be positive
      Returns:
      Rayleigh number (dimensionless, non-negative)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a transport property is not positive

    • reynolds

      public static double reynolds(double density, double velocity, double diameterM, double dynamicViscosity)
      Reynolds number of the inlet jet during vessel filling.

      Re = rho * v * d / mu

      Parameters:
      density - gas density in kg/m^3; must be positive
      velocity - inlet jet velocity in m/s
      diameterM - inlet nozzle diameter in m; must be positive
      dynamicViscosity - dynamic viscosity mu in Pa.s; must be positive
      Returns:
      Reynolds number (dimensionless, non-negative)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a property is not positive

    • churchillChuNusselt

      public static double churchillChuNusselt(double rayleigh, double prandtl)
      Churchill-Chu Nusselt number for natural convection on a vertical surface, valid over the full Rayleigh range.

      Nu = (0.825 + 0.387 * Ra^(1/6) / [1 + (0.492/Pr)^(9/16)]^(8/27))^2

      Parameters:
      rayleigh - Rayleigh number; must be non-negative
      prandtl - Prandtl number; must be positive
      Returns:
      Nusselt number (dimensionless)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are out of range

    • woodfieldFillingNusselt

      public static double woodfieldFillingNusselt(double reynoldsInletJet, double rayleighVesselHeight)
      Woodfield mixed-convection Nusselt number for a vessel being charged (filled) with gas.

      Nu = 0.56 * Re_d^0.67 + 0.104 * Ra_H^0.352

      where Re_d is the inlet-jet Reynolds number based on the nozzle diameter and Ra_H is the Rayleigh number based on the vessel internal height. The correlation was derived from charging experiments with hydrogen, nitrogen and argon.

      Parameters:
      reynoldsInletJet - inlet-jet Reynolds number based on nozzle diameter; must be non-negative
      rayleighVesselHeight - Rayleigh number based on vessel internal height; must be non-negative
      Returns:
      Nusselt number (dimensionless)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if an input is negative

    • heatTransferCoefficient

      public static double heatTransferCoefficient(double nusselt, double thermalConductivity, double characteristicLengthM)
      Convective heat-transfer coefficient from a Nusselt number.

      h = Nu * k / L

      Parameters:
      nusselt - Nusselt number; must be non-negative
      thermalConductivity - gas thermal conductivity k in W/(m.K); must be positive
      characteristicLengthM - characteristic length L in m; must be positive
      Returns:
      heat-transfer coefficient in W/(m^2.K)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are out of range

    • rohsenowNucleateBoilingHeatFlux

      public static double rohsenowNucleateBoilingHeatFlux(double liquidDynamicViscosity, double latentHeat, double liquidDensity, double vaporDensity, double surfaceTension, double liquidSpecificHeat, double wallSuperheatK, double prandtlLiquid, double surfaceFluidConstant, double prandtlExponent)
      Rohsenow nucleate pool-boiling heat flux at a wetted, fire-heated wall.

      q'' = mu_l * h_fg * sqrt(g * (rho_l - rho_v) / sigma) * [ cp_l * dTe / (Csf * h_fg * Pr_l^s) ]^3 where dTe = T_wall - T_sat is the wall superheat. A wall superheat of zero or below returns zero flux. The Prandtl exponent s is 1.0 for water and 1.7 for other fluids per Rohsenow's original recommendation.

      Parameters:
      liquidDynamicViscosity - liquid dynamic viscosity in Pa·s; must be positive
      latentHeat - latent heat of vaporization in J/kg; must be positive
      liquidDensity - saturated-liquid density in kg/m³; must be positive
      vaporDensity - saturated-vapor density in kg/m³; must be non-negative and below the liquid density
      surfaceTension - liquid-vapor surface tension in N/m; must be positive
      liquidSpecificHeat - liquid specific heat at constant pressure in J/(kg·K); must be positive
      wallSuperheatK - wall superheat T_wall - T_sat in K
      prandtlLiquid - liquid Prandtl number; must be positive
      surfaceFluidConstant - Rohsenow surface-fluid constant Csf (typically 0.006 to 0.013); must be positive
      prandtlExponent - Prandtl exponent s (1.0 for water, 1.7 otherwise); must be positive
      Returns:
      nucleate boiling heat flux in W/m²
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is out of range
